rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees involving ports, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to avoid introducing undefined references.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports only when they are visible in the current scope.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers unless they exist in the analyzed model.
Ensure that AGREE expressions use types compatible with the AADL data model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type contracts should use visible feature names and constants from the model.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not assume that AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: Defensive rules for type mismatches and property references
Pattern library examples for range monitoring and output envelopes
